title: santalum album
intro: Santalum album, also known as East Indian sandalwood, is a species of evergreen tree native to India and Southeast Asia, valued for its fragrant heartwood. The medicinal properties of Santalum album are attributed to its essential oils, which contain sesquiterpenes, specifically santalol, responsible for its therapeutic benefits. The health benefits of East Indian sandalwood include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which can aid in soothing skin irritations, reducing stress, and promoting relaxation. East Indian sandalwood is commonly used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, powders, and teas, often employed in aromatherapy, perfumery, and traditional medicine.
